CREATE TABLE dates_table (event_name VARCHAR, event_date DATE);
INSERT INTO dates_table VALUES ('Event A', DATE '1999-12-31');
INSERT INTO dates_table VALUES ('Event B', DATE '2000-01-01');
INSERT INTO dates_table VALUES ('Event C', DATE '2010-05-20');
INSERT INTO dates_table VALUES ('Event D', DATE '2001-06-15');
SELECT event_name, millennium(event_date) AS millennium FROM dates_table;
SELECT COUNT(*), millennium(event_date) AS millennium FROM dates_table GROUP BY millennium(event_date);
SELECT event_name FROM dates_table WHERE millennium(event_date) = 3;
SELECT DISTINCT millennium(event_date) FROM dates_table ORDER BY millennium(event_date);
CREATE TABLE another_table (event_id INT, event_date2 DATE);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, DATE '1999-01-01'), (2, DATE '2002-07-14'), (3, DATE '2015-03-30');

SELECT a.event_name, a.event_date, millennium(a.event_date) AS mill_a, millennium(b.event_date2) AS mill_b FROM dates_table a JOIN another_table b ON millennium(a.event_date) = millennium(b.event_date2);
WITH mill_data AS (SELECT event_name, millennium(event_date) AS mill FROM dates_table) SELECT mill, COUNT(*) AS total_events FROM mill_data GROUP BY mill;
SELECT a.event_name FROM dates_table a LEFT JOIN another_table b ON millennium(a.event_date) = millennium(b.event_date2) WHERE b.event_id IS NULL;
